fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alabama Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alabama - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity alabama, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to alabama, speech-language pathologist compact alabama, alabama speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Alabama Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.physical_address: 100 N. Union St., Suite 724, Montgomery, AL 36130

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Alabama. Requires a master's degree, clinical fellowship, and passing Praxis score.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship. Allows supervised practice while fulfilling post-graduate experience requirements.

steps.title: Earn a Master's Degree in SLP
steps.description: Complete a master's or doctoral degree in speech-language pathology from a CAA-accredited program.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331). The current passing score is 162.

steps.title: Complete Clinical Fellowship
steps.description: Complete a minimum of 36 weeks of full-time supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours) under an ASHA-certified SLP.

steps.title: Submit Application to ABESPA
steps.description: Submit the application with the $100 fee plus $50 license fee. Include official transcripts, Praxis scores, CF verification, and background check.

documents.detail: From a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.
Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.
CF supervisor attestation or ASHA CCC-SLP certificate.
Criminal background check as required by ABESPA.

telepractice_text: Alabama permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. The compact allows SLPs licensed in member states to practice across state lines through a compact privilege. Alabama-licensed SLPs must comply with state telehealth regulations.
provisional_text: Alabama issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship. This allows supervised practice while fulfilling the 36-week post-graduate experience requirement.
reciprocity_text: Alabama is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact, which allows SLPs licensed in other compact member states to obtain a compact privilege to practice in Alabama. Traditional endorsement pathways are also available for non-compact applicants.

faqs.question: How much does an Alabama SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is approximately $150, including a $100 application fee and $50 license fee. Biennial renewal is $100.

faqs.question: Is Alabama part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Alabama has enacted ASLP-IC legislation. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Alabama through a compact privilege once the system is fully operational.

faqs.question: Does Alabama allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Alabama permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Alabama license or ASLP compact privilege.

faqs.question: Does Alabama have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Alabama participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Alabama without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Alabama?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alabama while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Alabama is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact, which allows SLPs licensed in other compact member states to obtain a compact privilege to practice in Alabama. Traditional endorsement pathways are also available for non-compact applicants.
